taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 544 — Loss-calculation cross-references

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 544, subsections (1) and (2)
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: For section 543, trade, property-business and miscellaneous-transaction losses are calculated under the specified ITTOIA 2005 loss provisions.
applicabilityConditions: A charitable trust loss falls within a section 543 category.
The corresponding ITTOIA 2005 calculation provision must be applied to the relevant activity.
doesNotProve: It does not prove that a loss exists or calculate its amount.
It is not a finding about a charity, transaction, return, assessment or person.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 544 — Miscellaneous-transaction definition

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 544, subsection (3)
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: A miscellaneous transaction is one whose hypothetical income or gains, ignoring section 527, would be charged under a provision to which section 1016 applies and for which the trustees would be liable.
applicabilityConditions: The transaction is tested on the statutory hypothetical-income-or-gains basis.
The section 527 exemption is ignored for that test.
The trustees would have been liable for the hypothetical charge.
doesNotProve: It does not prove that a transaction occurred outside charitable purposes or that tax is due.
It is not a finding about a charity, transaction, return, assessment or person.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 545 — Expenditure scope for section 543(1)(f)

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 545
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: For section 543(1)(f), expenditure includes capital expenditure but excludes investing trust funds, making a loan and repaying all or part of a borrowing.
applicabilityConditions: The classification is being made under section 543(1)(f).
Any investment, loan or borrowing repayment must be tested under the other applicable statutory limbs.
doesNotProve: An exclusion from section 543(1)(f) does not establish exclusion from every non-charitable-expenditure category.
It is not a finding about a charity, transaction, return, assessment or person.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 546 — Commitment-related expenditure timing

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 546
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: Expenditure referable to a commitment entered before or during a tax year is treated as incurred in that year if hypothetical tax-year accounts under UK generally accepted accounting practice would take it into account.
applicabilityConditions: The expenditure is referable to a contractual or non-contractual commitment entered before or during the tax year.
UK generally accepted accounting practice would require the expenditure in hypothetical accounts for that tax year.
doesNotProve: It does not determine the accounting treatment, commitment date or expenditure amount for a case.
It is not a payment-date-only rule or a finding about liability.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 547 — Overseas-payment reasonable-steps restriction

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 547
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: A payment made or to be made to a body outside the UK is non-charitable expenditure under section 543(1)(f), despite being incurred for charitable purposes only, if the trustees did not take the steps HMRC considers reasonable to ensure charitable application.
applicabilityConditions: The payment is made or to be made to a body outside the United Kingdom.
It is incurred for charitable purposes only.
The trustees' assurance steps are tested against HMRC's statutory reasonable-steps judgment.
doesNotProve: It does not determine which steps HMRC would consider reasonable or whether the overseas body misapplied funds.
It does not prove misconduct, tax due or loss of charity status.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 548 — Same-year investment and loan recycling

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 548
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: Where a non-approved investment made in a tax year is realised, or a non-approved loan made in that year is repaid, further investment or lending in the same year up to the original sum is not non-charitable expenditure merely under section 543(1)(i) or (j).
applicabilityConditions: The original investment or loan and its realisation or repayment occur in the same tax year.
The further investment or lending occurs in that tax year.
The protected amount does not exceed the sum originally invested or lent.
doesNotProve: It does not protect an amount above the original sum or prevent another non-charitable-expenditure limb from applying.
It does not calculate a case-specific protected amount.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 558 — Listed-investment purpose gateway

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 558, subsections (1)(a) and (2)
Section 55(11): amendments apply to investments made on or after 6 April 2026
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: For an investment made on or after 6 April 2026, a listed Type 1 to Type 11 investment is an approved charitable investment only if it is also made for an allowable purpose.
applicabilityConditions: The investment was made on or after 6 April 2026.
It is of a type listed in section 558(2).
It satisfies the separate allowable-purpose test in section 558(4).
doesNotProve: A listed type does not by itself establish an allowable purpose or approval for a pre-6-April-2026 investment.
It does not prove tax treatment, misconduct or HMRC agreement in a case.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 558 — Unlisted-investment claim route

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 558, subsections (1)(b) and (3)
Section 55(11): amendments apply to investments made on or after 6 April 2026
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: For an investment made on or after 6 April 2026 that is not a listed type, an HMRC officer may approve the loan or other investment on a claim if satisfied that it is made for an allowable purpose.
applicabilityConditions: The investment was made on or after 6 April 2026.
It is not of a type listed in section 558(2).
A claim is made and an HMRC officer is satisfied that the allowable-purpose test is met.
doesNotProve: It does not prove that a claim was made, that HMRC approved it or what claim form, channel or appeal route applies.
It is not an automatic approval or a finding about a charity or investment.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 558 — Allowable-purpose test

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 558, subsection (4)
Section 55(11): amendments apply to investments made on or after 6 April 2026
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: For investments made on or after 6 April 2026, an allowable purpose exists only where all the circumstances reasonably support sole benefit to the charitable trust, or that benefit plus ancillary or incidental purposes, and the investment is not made for tax avoidance.
applicabilityConditions: The investment was made on or after 6 April 2026.
All circumstances support the required trust-benefit purpose.
Any additional purpose is ancillary or incidental and the investment is not made for tax avoidance by the trust or another person.
doesNotProve: It does not decide purpose, benefit, ancillary status or tax avoidance from sector data alone.
It does not govern an earlier investment or establish HMRC approval.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 559 — Eligible security types

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 559
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: The section identifies government, international-entity, mutual-institution, open-ended-investment-company and listed or unlisted company securities capable of entering the section 558 Type 1 route, subject to section 560.
applicabilityConditions: The instrument is a security within the section's inclusive definitions.
It falls within a listed issuer class.
Any applicable section 560 conditions and section 558 purpose requirements are separately satisfied.
doesNotProve: Issuer classification alone does not establish approval, market conditions or an allowable purpose.
The unresolved legal effect of the retained Directive 2003/48/EC Annex references is not decided here.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 560 — Security Conditions A, B and C

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 560
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: Specified section 559 securities must meet the applicable exchange or quotation Condition A, paid-up Condition B and, for an unlisted incorporated company, capital-and-dividend Condition C, subject to the limited money-market and predecessor-company rules.
applicabilityConditions: The section 559 issuer category determines which conditions apply.
Security, market, paid-up, capital and dividend facts are tested on the statutory investment-day basis.
A supervised money market can disapply Conditions A and B but does not disapply Condition C where C applies.
doesNotProve: It does not establish exchange recognition, quotation, paid-up status, capital, dividends or a currency conversion for a case.
It does not by itself satisfy section 558's post-2026 allowable-purpose test.

taxRules.name: Income Tax Act 2007, section 561 — Approved charitable loan gateway

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 561
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: An approved charitable loan must not be made by way of investment and must be a solely charitable loan to another charity, a beneficiary-purpose loan, qualifying current-account money or a loan HMRC approves on claim as benefiting the trust and not made for tax avoidance.
applicabilityConditions: The loan is not made by way of investment.
At least one statutory Condition B route is met.
The fourth route additionally requires a claim and HMRC officer satisfaction.
doesNotProve: It does not prove the loan's purpose, beneficiary status, bank arrangement, trust benefit, absence of avoidance or HMRC approval.
It does not establish a claim form, decision notice or appeal route.

taxRules.name: Corporation Tax Act 2010, section 497 — Loss-calculation cross-references

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 497, subsections (1) and (3)
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: For section 496, trade, property-business and miscellaneous-transaction losses are calculated under the specified CTA 2009 loss provisions.
applicabilityConditions: A charitable company loss falls within a section 496 category.
The corresponding CTA 2009 calculation provision must be applied to the relevant activity.
doesNotProve: It does not prove that a loss exists or calculate its amount.
It is not a finding about a charity, transaction, return, assessment or person.

taxRules.name: Corporation Tax Act 2010, section 497 — Miscellaneous-transaction definition

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 497, subsection (2)
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: A miscellaneous transaction is one whose hypothetical income or gains, ignoring section 481, would be charged to corporation tax under or by virtue of a provision to which section 1173 applies.
applicabilityConditions: The transaction is tested on the statutory hypothetical-income-or-gains basis.
The section 481 exemption is ignored for that test.
The hypothetical charge arises under or by virtue of a provision to which section 1173 applies.
doesNotProve: It does not prove that a transaction occurred outside charitable purposes or that corporation tax is due.
It is not a finding about a charity, transaction, return, assessment or person.

Any investment, loan or borrowing repayment must be tested under the other applicable statutory limbs.
doesNotProve: An exclusion from section 496(1)(d) does not establish exclusion from every non-charitable-expenditure category.
It is not a finding about a charity, transaction, return, assessment or person.

It is not a payment-date-only rule or a finding about liability.

doesNotProve: Issuer classification alone does not establish approval, market conditions or an allowable purpose.
The unresolved legal effect of the retained Directive 2003/48/EC Annex references is not decided here.

taxRules.name: Corporation Tax Act 2010, section 513 — Security Conditions A, B and C

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 513
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: Specified section 512 securities must meet the applicable exchange or quotation Condition A, paid-up Condition B and, for an unlisted incorporated company, capital-and-dividend Condition C, subject to the limited money-market and predecessor-company rules.
applicabilityConditions: The section 512 issuer category determines which conditions apply.
Security, market, paid-up, capital and dividend facts are tested on the statutory investment-day basis.
A supervised money market can disapply Conditions A and B but does not disapply Condition C where C applies.
doesNotProve: It does not establish exchange recognition, quotation, paid-up status, capital, dividends or a currency conversion for a case.
It does not by itself satisfy section 511's post-2026 allowable-purpose test.

taxRules.name: Corporation Tax Act 2010, section 514 — Approved charitable loan gateway

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 514
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.ruleSummary: An approved charitable loan must not be made by way of investment and must be a solely charitable loan to another charity, a beneficiary-purpose loan, qualifying current-account money or a loan HMRC approves on claim as benefiting the company and not made for tax avoidance.
applicabilityConditions: The loan is not made by way of investment.
At least one statutory Condition B route is met.
The fourth route additionally requires a claim and HMRC officer satisfaction.
doesNotProve: It does not prove the loan's purpose, beneficiary status, bank arrangement, company benefit, absence of avoidance or HMRC approval.
It does not establish a claim form, decision notice or appeal route.

taxRules.name: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992, section 256 — Charitable-gain exemption

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 256(1)
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.citation: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 s 256
taxRules.ruleSummary: Subject to the following provisions of section 256, a gain is not a chargeable gain if it accrues to a charity and is applicable and applied for charitable purposes.
applicabilityConditions: The gain accrues to a charity.
The gain is applicable for charitable purposes.
The gain is applied for charitable purposes.
The restrictions in the following provisions of section 256 are separately considered.
doesNotProve: It does not prove charitable application, charitable use or entitlement to the exemption in a particular case.
It does not calculate a gain, tax liability or non-exempt amount.

taxRules.name: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992, section 256 — Cessation of charitable trusts

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 256(2)
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.citation: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 s 256
taxRules.ruleSummary: If property held on charitable trusts ceases to be subject to those trusts, the trustees are treated as disposing of and immediately reacquiring it at market value, with the disposal gain treated as not accruing to a charity; the section also removes charitable treatment from traced gains and provides a three-year assessment window for tax chargeable through that tracing rule.
applicabilityConditions: Property is held on charitable trusts.
The property ceases to be subject to charitable trusts.
For the tracing limb, the property directly or indirectly represents consideration for a disposal of assets by the trustees.
doesNotProve: It does not establish that property ceased to be subject to charitable trusts, trace consideration or determine market value in a case.
It does not itself prove a valid assessment, calculate capital gains tax or establish collection consequences.

taxRules.name: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992, section 256 — Attributed gains treated as chargeable

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 256(3), (3A) and (4)
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.citation: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 s 256
taxRules.ruleSummary: Where a charitable trust has an Income Tax Act 2007 section 540 non-exempt amount for a tax year, or a charitable company has a Corporation Tax Act 2010 section 493 non-exempt amount for an accounting period, gains for that period are treated as being, and always having been, chargeable gains so far as attributed to that amount under section 256A or 256C.
applicabilityConditions: The trust or company has the relevant statutory non-exempt amount for the period.
A gain accrues to it in that tax year or accounting period.
The gain is attributed to the non-exempt amount under section 256A or section 256C.
doesNotProve: It does not prove that a non-exempt amount exists or that a particular gain was validly attributed.
It does not calculate the chargeable gain, tax due or procedural validity of an HMRC action.

taxRules.name: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992, section 256A — Trust gain attribution

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 256A
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.citation: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 s 256A
taxRules.ruleSummary: A charitable trust's attributable gains for a tax year may be attributed to its Income Tax Act 2007 section 540 non-exempt amount only while that amount remains unused, and the whole amount must be exhausted through attributable gains, attributable income under section 541, or a combination of both.
applicabilityConditions: The charitable trust has a section 540 non-exempt amount for the tax year.
Attributable gains exist for that tax year.
Attribution does not exceed the unused non-exempt amount.
The non-exempt amount is wholly used by attributable gains, attributable income or both.
doesNotProve: It does not identify which gains are attributable or specify them under section 256B.
It does not calculate the non-exempt amount, attributable income, gain or capital gains tax.

taxRules.name: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992, section 256B — Trust gain specification

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 256B
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.citation: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 s 256B
taxRules.ruleSummary: Trustees may specify by notice to an HMRC officer which attributable gains are attributed to the non-exempt amount; an HMRC officer may determine the gains only after requiring a specification and receiving no trustees' notice within the 30-day period beginning with the requirement day.
applicabilityConditions: The attribution is under section 256A.
For trustees' specification, notice is given to an HMRC officer.
For an HMRC determination, an officer first requires a specification and no notice is given before the 30-day period ends.
doesNotProve: It does not prove that HMRC made a requirement, that a notice or determination was valid, or when it was received.
It does not establish a notice form, service channel, amendment route or appeal right.

taxRules.name: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992, section 256C — Company gain attribution

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 256C
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.citation: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 s 256C
taxRules.ruleSummary: A charitable company's attributable gains for an accounting period may be attributed to its Corporation Tax Act 2010 section 493 non-exempt amount only while that amount remains unused, and the whole amount must be exhausted through attributable gains, attributable income under section 494, or a combination of both.
applicabilityConditions: The charitable company has a section 493 non-exempt amount for the accounting period.
Attributable gains exist for that accounting period.
Attribution does not exceed the unused non-exempt amount.
The non-exempt amount is wholly used by attributable gains, attributable income or both.
doesNotProve: It does not identify which gains are attributable or specify them under section 256D.
It does not calculate the non-exempt amount, attributable income, gain or corporation tax.

taxRules.name: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992, section 256D — Company gain specification

evidence.locator: Current consolidated section 256D
TaxSorted exact treatment-field and authority-selector mapping
TaxSorted labels the normalised supplementary-law summary

taxRules.citation: Taxation of Chargeable Gains Act 1992 s 256D
taxRules.ruleSummary: A charitable company may specify by notice to an HMRC officer which attributable gains are attributed to the non-exempt amount; an HMRC officer may determine the gains only after requiring a specification and receiving no company notice within the 30-day period beginning with the requirement day.
applicabilityConditions: The attribution is under section 256C.
For company specification, notice is given to an HMRC officer.
For an HMRC determination, an officer first requires a specification and no notice is given before the 30-day period ends.
doesNotProve: It does not prove that HMRC made a requirement, that a notice or determination was valid, or when it was received.
It does not establish a notice form, service channel, amendment route or appeal right.

transparencyGaps.name: Substantial-donor repeals retain a conditional legacy-contract branch

evidence.locator: Schedule 3 paragraph 27(2) and (3): post-1-April-2013 transactions, pre-date contracts and post-date variations
Schedule 3 paragraph 29: separate charitable-trust transition from 1 April 2011
Schedule 3 paragraph 30: separate charitable-company transition from 1 April 2011

transparencyGaps.title: Former ITA 2007 sections 549–557 and CTA 2010 sections 502–510 are not ordinary current rules and are not safely erased
transparencyGaps.detail: The substantial-donor ranges are repealed for ordinary current transactions. Finance Act 2011 Schedule 3 paragraph 27 nevertheless excludes from the relevant post-1-April-2013 repeal a transaction entered into under a contract made before 1 April 2013, unless the transaction is under a contract variation made on or after that date. Paragraphs 29 and 30 separately modify the former trust and company regimes from 1 April 2011. This supplement does not admit the repealed ranges as ordinary current tax rules; it records the exact selector for a later conditional historical branch.
transparencyGaps.consequence: A client must collect the transaction date, original contract date, any variation date, taxpayer type and the paragraph 29 or 30 donor and taint facts before deciding whether former substantial-donor law can matter. It must not infer liability, misconduct or irrelevance from repeal status alone.
